checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 reference only AC-001 through AC-007, all of which exist in requirements.md and are concretely defined by TEST-001 through TEST-006 in acceptance-tests.md.
blockers_format_valid=true: T-001 uses literal None; T-002 through T-005 use comma-separated existing T-NNN identifiers only.
tasks.md Requirements fields cover every REQ-001 through REQ-011, and traceability.md independently maps all eleven requirements to T-001 through T-005.
AC-001 is assigned to T-001, AC-002 through AC-004 to T-002/T-003, AC-005 to T-004, and AC-006/AC-007 to T-005; traceability.md records the same coverage.
Every task T-001 through T-005 has at least one REQ-NNN in its Requirements field.
No task is an exclusively test-file task or has a title containing test, spec, or verify; all verification work is directly tied to feature implementation and references the applicable AC-NNN.
In attempt 1 round 2, every task T-001 through T-005 remains Approval: Draft and Status: Planned; no task is human-approved prematurely.
T-001, T-004, and T-005 declare medium/acceptance-first; T-002 and T-003 declare high/tdd. These pairings match the risk-gate matrix, and precheck workflow_match_precheck is PASS.
The only shared target is AC-002: T-002 explicitly covers implementation-agent snapshot/context isolation, while T-003 explicitly covers reviewer/evaluator isolation, providing distinct scopes.
Every blocker reference resolves to one of dependency-graph.json nodes T-001 through T-005; no blocker references a nonexistent task.
BLOCKERS-FORMAT passed, and the graph edges terminate through T-001 without revisiting a node; no dependency cycle exists.

T-001 routing, T-002 task context isolation, T-003 review/evaluator isolation, T-004 handoff metrics, and T-005 release/rollback each define one coherent concern; associated tests and rollback evidence are directly tied verification or mandatory release housekeeping.

Each task names inspectable commands or artifacts: TEST-001, T-002/T-003 red-green logs and implementation reports, TEST-004 and retrospective fields, or TEST-005/TEST-006 plus run-all and repository validators. Outcomes are binary and measurable.

traceability.md contains T-001 through T-005 and only existing REQ-001 through REQ-011. Every requirement row supplies a specific 'N/A — cross-layer only' rationale, and Layer Coverage separately identifies infrastructure and security applicability against the hash-bound layer specifications.
